authoritative_sources.title: OpenRTB API Specification Version 2.6
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB Tech Lab maintains OpenRTB as a foundational public programmatic advertising protocol for bid requests, impressions, supply-chain objects, creative attributes, and exchange terminology used in ad-tech interface triage.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Programmatic interface intake, bidstream dependency triage, and source terminology normalization
used_for: OpenRTB bid request, response, impression, site, app, video, audio, native, and marketplace terminology
interface dependency evidence for DSP, SSP, exchange, and publisher-platform handoffs
source hierarchy when operational claims involve bidstream fields or integration payloads
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to artifact-aware triage and source evidence for programmatic interface issues rather than generic IT support language.

authoritative_sources.title: Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) 4.3
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB Tech Lab VAST is the public industry specification for video ad serving responses, wrappers, media files, tracking events, verification resources, and VAST error handling used in video ad-tech operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Video ad serving, tracking, wrapper, verification, and VAST error exception handling
used_for: VAST tag, wrapper, media file, tracking event, verification, and error evidence
video ad-tech incident and rework state definitions
launch validation and completion evidence requirements for video ad delivery
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires VAST failures to be handled as artifact-specific exceptions with owner routing and retest evidence before closure.

authoritative_sources.title: Sellers.json and OpenRTB SupplyChain Object
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB Tech Lab sellers.json and OpenRTB SupplyChain Object guidance define public supply-chain transparency artifacts, seller identifiers, and buyer-side verification workflows for programmatic ad operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Supply-chain transparency, seller-ID evidence, and inventory legitimacy exception handling
used_for: sellers.json, seller ID, schain node, ads.txt, app-ads.txt, and bid-request supply-chain evidence
inventory-legitimacy and supply-chain mismatch exception handling
owner routing when seller records, exchange logs, and platform records disagree
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes seller-ID and supply-chain mismatches operational exception states that require source evidence and escalation rather than informal override.

authoritative_sources.title: Global Privacy Platform Technical Specifications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB Tech Lab stewards the Global Privacy Platform specifications for consent and preference signaling across the digital advertising supply chain, making it authoritative for privacy-signal artifact handling and escalation boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Privacy-signal transport, consent-string dependency handling, and data-use escalation
used_for: GPP or consent-string artifact handling
privacy-signal outage or fallback escalation
boundary between operational signal routing and privacy-law interpretation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Allows the lane to inspect and route consent-signal evidence while refusing to interpret legal adequacy or approve privacy fallbacks.

authoritative_sources.title: Standard Terms and Conditions for Internet Advertising for Media Buys One Year or Less, Version 3.0
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB and 4A's standard terms are widely used public contract workflow guidance for digital advertising and define IOs, ad materials, delivery, third-party serving, measurement, discrepancies, data use, billing, cancellation, and makegood boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Client engagement, IO, delivery obligation, discrepancy, data-use, and contractual boundary handling
used_for: client delivery artifact and approval expectations
delivery discrepancy, makegood, billing, and data-use escalation boundaries
distinguishing operational status packaging from contractual signoff
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to evidence-backed status and handoff when a request touches client commitments, IO terms, delivery obligations, service credits, or makegoods.

authoritative_sources.title: Incident Response Recommendations and Considerations for Cybersecurity Risk Management: A CSF 2.0 Community Profile
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3 is official U.S. public guidance for incident response preparation, detection, analysis, containment, recovery, communication, and lessons learned, supporting incident lifecycle and evidence expectations for ad-tech service operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Incident preparation, detection, analysis, response coordination, recovery, communication, and closeout evidence
used_for: incident lifecycle states and severity-aware triage
impact, owner, containment, recovery, communication, and lessons-learned evidence
routing when an incident crosses security, engineering, or executive authority
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines why incident closure requires evidence of impact, owner action, validation, communication, and follow-up rather than unsupported closure.

authoritative_sources.title: Guide for Security-Focused Configuration Management of Information Systems
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-128 is official public guidance for configuration management planning, change control, impact analysis, monitoring, and evidence needed before production technology changes are treated as ready.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Change-readiness, configuration impact analysis, testing, approval, rollback, monitoring, and post-change evidence
used_for: change request prerequisite validation
testing, rollback, approval, monitoring, and configuration evidence
refusal triggers for unapproved production changes
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes missing test, rollback, impact, or approval evidence a blocker for ad-tech connector, tag, API, or configuration changes.

authoritative_sources.title: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 provides recognized public controls for access control, configuration management, audit logging, incident response, system integrity, and privacy controls that limit autonomous access, change, and confidentiality handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Access-readiness, audit trail, configuration control, confidentiality, and privacy-control boundary handling
used_for: access request evidence and least-privilege owner routing
audit log and retention expectations
confidentiality and security exception refusal triggers
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from granting access or overriding security controls while allowing evidence-backed access-readiness packaging.

authoritative_sources.title: Invalid Traffic Detection and Filtration Standards Addendum Update
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Media Rating Council publishes recognized advertising measurement standards and accreditation criteria; the IVT addendum defines invalid-traffic evidence, data completeness, reporting, confidentiality, and discrepancy handling expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Measurement quality, IVT, discrepancy, reporting, confidentiality, and quality-review escalation
used_for: invalid-traffic, viewability, discrepancy, and measurement-quality evidence handling
data completeness and reporting caveats
escalation boundaries when quality or measurement conclusions require specialist review
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires measurement-quality issues to be treated as evidence and escalation packages rather than unsupported performance or causality claims.

authoritative_sources.title: Advertising and Marketing Basics
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator for deceptive advertising; its public guidance marks claims, disclosures, endorsements, native advertising, and consumer-protection questions as legal or policy review boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Regulated advertising content, claim, disclosure, endorsement, and legal-review boundary handling
used_for: legal and editorial refusal triggers
claim, disclosure, endorsement, native-ad, and consumer-protection escalation
distinguishing operational routing from legal or policy approval
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents ad-tech operations from approving regulated content or disclosure choices while allowing it to package affected artifacts for review.

authoritative_sources.title: Network Advertising Initiative Principles and Self-Regulatory Framework
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NAI is a recognized advertising self-regulatory body for interest-based advertising and defines privacy, choice, sensitive data, accountability, and data-use expectations relevant to audience-data and ad-tech operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Audience-data, consumer-choice, sensitive-data, accountability, and confidentiality escalation
used_for: audience-data and sensitive-data handling caveats
consumer-choice and approved-use escalation
privacy and confidentiality refusal triggers
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires audience-data, targeting, choice, and data-use requests to be routed when tenant facts or privacy authority are missing.

workflow_stages.name: Ad-tech operations intake and authority prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm tenant service model, client or product scope, platform, environment, affected interface, work type, incident severity, access owner, change owner, approval rules, confidentiality constraints, and systems of record before accepting execution.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Truth-set assembly for tickets, systems, interfaces, and client artifacts
workflow_stages.description: Assemble tickets, engagement records, logs, ad-tech payloads, tags, pixels, GPP or consent-string artifacts, supply-chain records, runbooks, approval records, client communications, and retrieval locations into an auditable evidence set.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Incident, access, and exception triage
workflow_stages.description: Classify incidents, access requests, VAST or tag failures, API errors, supply-chain mismatches, consent-signal gaps, measurement anomalies, and confidentiality conflicts into lifecycle states, owners, impact, and next actions without claiming unowned authority.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Change-readiness and integration-risk handling
workflow_stages.description: Validate change ticket, affected systems, field mapping, test evidence, rollback plan, monitoring plan, change window, approvals, client impact, and data-flow constraints before treating an ad-tech connector, tag, pixel, API, privacy-signal, or content workflow change as ready.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Client, content, confidentiality, and approval-aware delivery
workflow_stages.description: Apply engagement scope, approved recipient, approved delivery channel, client communication, audience-data, operational-log, platform-credential, unreleased-creative, and content approval checks before producing or sending operational outputs.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Validation, completion evidence, and closeout
workflow_stages.description: Capture what changed or recovered, who owned it, what evidence validates the outcome, what client communication occurred, what rollback or follow-up remains, and where the completion record is retained before marking work complete.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: Tenant operating policy, service model, approval matrix, escalation threshold, confidentiality rules, quality standards, change window rules, and rollback expectations
Client engagement scope, statement of work, IO or contract references, client commitments, business unit, product, advertiser, publisher, jurisdiction, and approved communication channels
Project plan, ticket, incident report, access request, change request, runbook, release note, QA checklist, owner map, reviewer map, and approval records
Ad server, DSP, SSP, exchange, publisher portal, consent platform, content system, measurement system, verification vendor, delivery system, CRM, document management, and audit-log references

OpenRTB bid request or response samples, VAST tags, VAST error logs, tag wrappers, pixels, macros, API payloads, webhook events, batch-sync files, GPP or consent strings, ads.txt or app-ads.txt entries, sellers.json records, schain nodes, seller IDs, deal IDs, and event logs

Access-entitlement evidence, service account or API key owner record, least-privilege justification, security-review record, monitoring alert, incident severity record, impact summary, containment or recovery note, and validation evidence

Test results, retest evidence, rollback plan, change approval, implementation record, post-change monitoring evidence, client communication log, exception queue note, escalation note, completion record, and retention location
systems_of_record: document management and client file system
content and creative asset management system
ad server and campaign delivery platform
DSP, SSP, exchange, publisher portal, and partner integration systems
measurement, verification, viewability, invalid-traffic, brand-safety, and analytics systems
identity, access, service-account, API-key, and secrets-management systems
source control, release, configuration, monitoring, and observability systems
client communication, approval, secure-delivery, audit-log, and retention systems

allowed: Determine whether documented ad-tech operations evidence supports moving work to accepted intake, investigating, awaiting evidence, ready for change, scheduled, blocked, rework, escalated, implemented-by-owner, validating, completed, or closed.

Prepare evidence-backed execution status updates, incident summaries, access-readiness notes, change-readiness packages, integration-risk escalations, exception queue notes, and completion evidence records.

Normalize ad-tech terms and artifacts such as OpenRTB, VAST, schain, sellers.json, ads.txt, GPP strings, tag wrappers, pixels, API payloads, service accounts, event logs, and measurement records into lifecycle state and owner routing.
escalate: The applicable tenant policy, platform policy, client scope, jurisdiction, contract term, approval owner, security control, privacy obligation, or system record is missing and would change the workflow.

Ticket, logs, client records, platform records, OpenRTB or VAST artifacts, supply-chain records, consent-signal records, measurement evidence, access records, test results, or approval evidence disagree materially.

Legal claims, disclosures, endorsements, native-ad treatment, audience-data use, consent fallback, access-control exception, production engineering decision, client rights, or professional judgment determines the answer.

Incident impact, service credits, makegoods, delivery commitments, contractual obligations, security exceptions, privacy posture, or final client acceptance requires account, finance, legal, privacy, security, engineering, or executive approval.

A confidentiality, approved-recipient, approved-channel, audience-data, platform-credential, operational-log, unreleased-creative, client-rights, or data-security conflict affects delivery or use of information.
refuse: Requests to approve legal advice, privacy-law interpretation, editorial content, regulated advertising claims, security policy exceptions, production engineering changes, financial credits, contractual exceptions, or final client acceptance.

Requests to grant access, approve a change, launch an integration, mark an incident resolved, or close work without required ticket, owner approval, test, rollback, validation, client communication, audit, or retention evidence.

Requests to suppress exceptions, bypass access controls, skip rollback planning, ignore conflicting source records, disclose restricted client data, or treat a tenant-specific assumption as established fact.

domain_failure_modes: A tag, pixel, VAST wrapper, OpenRTB payload, schain node, sellers.json record, ads.txt entry, API connector, webhook, batch sync, or consent-string artifact is missing, stale, malformed, or unsupported by logs.
Incident triage proceeds without severity, affected system, client impact, owner, containment, validation, communication, or closeout evidence.
Access-readiness packaging is confused with access approval, allowing unreviewed console, API key, service account, partner portal, or content-system privileges.
Change-readiness is claimed without impact analysis, testing, rollback plan, monitoring, implementation owner, approval evidence, or change window.
Supply-chain, measurement, IVT, viewability, discrepancy, or consent-signal issues are summarized as final conclusions without source evidence or specialist review.
Client files, audience data, operational logs, platform credentials, unreleased creative, or performance records are disclosed through an unapproved recipient, channel, purpose, or engagement scope.

Client instructions, tenant runbook, platform guidance, public standard, contract term, security control, and privacy obligation conflict and the lane synthesizes a confident answer instead of escalating.
The lane drifts into legal, editorial, security, engineering, privacy, financial, contractual, attestation, or final client signoff authority because the work sits in an execution queue.

scenario_family_coverage.notes: Scenarios block work when tickets, logs, approvals, tests, rollback plans, or authority-source context are missing.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
scenario_family_coverage.notes: Scenarios route legal, editorial, privacy, security, engineering, financial, contractual, and final approval decisions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
scenario_family_coverage.notes: Scenarios enforce approved recipient, channel, engagement scope, testing, rollback, and approval evidence before delivery or change progression.
Queue-level must-pass family covered by blocked intake, VAST or pixel rework, and low-confidence missing-record handling.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
scenario_family_coverage.notes: Queue-level must-pass family covered by VAST, pixel, consent-service, and conflicting-policy exceptions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
scenario_family_coverage.notes: Queue-level must-pass family covered by refusal to close change work without test, rollback, approval, and validation evidence.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: A system dependency fails and cross-team routing is required
scenario_family_coverage.notes: Queue-level must-pass family covered by consent-string service outage with SSP and exchange dependency routing.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: A requested change lacks testing, rollback, or approval evidence
scenario_family_coverage.notes: Queue-level must-pass family covered by OpenRTB field mapping and schain normalization change-readiness hold.

unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public controls define access-control posture, but exact role names and approval owners are tenant-specific and must be retrieved at runtime before acting.

Public ad-tech and regulator sources define boundary triggers, but the lane must route legal or privacy-law interpretation to the appropriate owner when jurisdiction or tenant policy changes the result.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Client contract remedies for delivery incidents
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: IAB/4A's terms support common workflow terminology, but actual service credits, makegoods, billing, or contract remedies remain client-contract and account or legal authority.

notes: Research satisfied the professional-and-information-services domain pack by covering professional standards, client-delivery workflow, and confidentiality or quality-review references.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because tenant-specific items are recorded as runtime prerequisites and refusal or escalation triggers.
The pack constrains the final spec and evaluation scenarios around incident, access, change-readiness, integration, confidentiality, approval, and completion-evidence behavior.
